source.title: SunSafe Travel Insurance — Product Disclosure Statement (synthetic)

sections.text: SunSafe Travel Insurance PDS. This document sets out what is and is not covered.
Overseas medical expenses are covered up to $10,000,000 for emergency treatment while travelling.
Baggage and personal effects are covered subject to a sub-limit of $1,000 per item and $5,000 in total.
Exclusions: claims arising from extreme sports, war, or pre-existing conditions are not payable.

ai_output: This policy is excellent value for money and the best choice for families. Overseas medical is covered up to $10,000,000. Individual lost items are covered up to $5,000 each.

claims.text: This policy is excellent value for money and the best choice for families.
claims.label: unsupported
claims.rationale: No value-for-money or suitability judgment exists anywhere in the PDS.

claims.text: Overseas medical is covered up to $10,000,000.
claims.label: grounded
claims.rationale: Section s4_2 states exactly this limit.

claims.text: Individual lost items are covered up to $5,000 each.
claims.label: partial
claims.rationale: Baggage is covered, but the per-item limit is $1,000, not $5,000; the claim overstates it.
